nodes.name: Waitākere Ranges
nodes.summary: The Waitākere Ranges is a mountain range in New Zealand.

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 森林
nodes.summary: 森林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联402种鸟类，包括疣鼻棲鴨、Blue-throated piping guan、White-throated piping guan等402种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.id: loc-约翰-弗里德里希-格梅林-8a33c9ca
nodes.name: 约翰·弗里德里希·格梅林
nodes.summary: 约翰·弗里德里希·格梅林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联64种鸟类，包括灰穴䳍、杂色穴䳍、葡萄胸鴨等64种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 新西蘭鳩
nodes.summary: 新西蘭鳩（Kererū，学名 Hemiphaga novaeseelandiae），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括猎捕压力、栖息地破坏。
locations: 约翰·弗里德里希·格梅林

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: Christchurch City Council
nodes.summary: Christchurch City Council是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括新西蘭鳩，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The kererū (Hemiphaga novaeseelandiae), also known as kūkupa (northern Māori dialects), New Zealand pigeon or wood pigeon, is a species of pigeon native to New Zealand

links.evidence: H.|last2=Brassey|first2=R.|date=2000|title=New Zealand pigeon (Hemiphaga novaeseelandiae) on Raoul Island, Kermadec Group|url=https://www.notornis.osnz.org.nz/system/files/Notornis_47_1_36.pdf|url-status=live|journal=Notornis|volume=47|issue=1|pages=36–38|doi=10.63172/383560hgaaii |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190216153219/http://notornis.osnz.org.nz/system/files/Notornis_47_1_36.pdf|archive-date=16 February 2019}} They can be found in a variety of habitats including in native lowland forest, scrub, the countryside, and city gardens and parks. The kererū lives in habitats ranging in altitude from coastal to montane.{{Cite journal|last1=Clout|first1=M

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: However, due to the decline in its population, hunting is illegal

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: thumb|right|Traffic sign in New Zealand cautioning drivers of nearby kererū|alt=yellow and black traffic sign depicting flying bird Kererū were numerous until the 1960s, but they have since come under threat from introduced mammalian species, hunting, habitat degradation, and poor reproductive success.{{cite book|last=Best|first=Elsdon|author-link=Elsdon Best|orig-date=1942|url=http://nzetc.victoria.ac.nz/tm/scholarly/name-427176.html|title=Forest lore of the Maori|date=1977|publisher=E.C
